description: Personality and cognitive interpretations - neurotransmitter function, behavioral traits, mental health predispositions

GG.interpretation: Rapid dopamine breakdown - stress resilient, better working memory under pressure
recommendations: Higher stress tolerance
Good performance under pressure
Standard stimulant response

AG.interpretation: Balanced dopamine levels - moderate stress response
recommendations: Moderate approach to stimulants and stress
Mindful of caffeine intake

AA.effect: Slow COMT (Worrier)
AA.interpretation: Slow dopamine breakdown - higher baseline dopamine, sensitive to stress
recommendations: Mindful caffeine intake
Prioritize relaxation techniques
Meditation beneficial
AA.theWhy: Your COMT variant means dopamine clears more slowly, leaving you more sensitive to stress and stimulants
AA.scienceSimplified: Your body's stress-response system stays active longer due to slower dopamine processing

CC.interpretation: Full brain-derived neurotrophic factor function - efficient activity-dependent release
recommendations: Standard cognitive function
Normal memory consolidation

CT.interpretation: Met carrier - impaired activity-dependent BDNF secretion, affects memory consolidation
recommendations: Regular aerobic exercise boosts BDNF
Omega-3 supplementation may help
Cognitive exercises

TT.interpretation: Met/Met - impaired neuroplasticity, reduced hippocampal function
recommendations: Prioritize regular exercise
Memory training activities
Consider omega-3s
TT.theWhy: Your BDNF variant reduces the brain's growth factor, impairing memory and learning capacity
TT.scienceSimplified: Your brain's fertilizer system is less effective, making neural connections harder to form

CC.interpretation: Standard alpha-2A receptor expression - normal stress response
recommendations: Standard stress management

GC.interpretation: Altered receptor expression may affect stress and blood pressure regulation
recommendations: Monitor stress response
Blood pressure awareness

GG.interpretation: May have increased stress reactivity, higher baseline anxiety
recommendations: Stress management techniques particularly important
Consider relaxation practices

AA.effect: High serotonin transporter expression
AA.interpretation: Long allele homozygote - efficient serotonin reuptake, resilience to stress
recommendations: Standard stress resilience
Normal SSRI response expected
Moderate stress sensitivity
Mindfulness practices beneficial

GG.interpretation: Short allele homozygote - reduced serotonin reuptake, increased anxiety vulnerability
recommendations: Higher stress sensitivity
Meditation and CBT particularly beneficial
May have slower SSRI response

CC.interpretation: Standard serotonin 2A receptor function
recommendations: Standard serotonin-related health

CT.interpretation: May affect serotonin signaling and psychiatric medication response
recommendations: Awareness of psychiatric medication sensitivity
Some atypical antipsychotics may be more effective

TT.interpretation: May significantly affect serotonin signaling, altered medication response
recommendations: Consult before starting serotonin medications
Different atypical antipsychotic response

TT.interpretation: Normal CLOCK function - typical sleep-wake cycle
recommendations: Standard sleep hygiene
Be mindful of sleep timing
Morning light exposure

CC.interpretation: C allele associated with delayed sleep phase, longer sleep duration
recommendations: Adjust schedule to natural tendencies where possible
Evening chronotype common

TT.interpretation: Standard circadian rhythm
recommendations: Consistent sleep schedule
Maintain regular sleep-wake times

CC.interpretation: May have longer circadian period, morning person tendency
recommendations: Bright light exposure in morning helpful

TT.interpretation: Efficient fatty acid binding in brain
recommendations: Standard cognitive function

TC.interpretation: Some reduction in fatty acid binding
recommendations: Omega-3 supplementation may support cognition

CC.interpretation: Associated with altered sleep quality and cognitive performance
recommendations: Prioritize sleep hygiene
Consider omega-3s for cognitive support

TT.interpretation: Normal function affects CNS medications
recommendations: Standard response to CNS drugs metabolized by CYP2D6

TC.interpretation: Reduced metabolism of some psychiatric medications
recommendations: May have altered response to certain antidepressants, opioids

CC.interpretation: Significantly reduced CYP2D6 activity
recommendations: Many psychiatric medications affected
Consult physician

11.interpretation: Two long alleles - high serotonin transporter
recommendations: Standard stress resilience
Moderate stress sensitivity
Increased anxiety vulnerability with stress
Therapy recommended

GG.interpretation: This is the common genotype at the ANKK1 Taq1A marker. A meta-analysis found genotype associations with striatal dopamine D2/3 receptor availability in healthy participants, but this marker does not determine personality, reward behavior, or addiction risk.
recommendations: Do not use this genotype to predict behavior or substance-use risk

AG.interpretation: One A1 allele has been associated with lower striatal dopamine D2/3 receptor availability in some healthy-control imaging studies. Evidence linking the marker to addiction phenotypes is inconsistent.
recommendations: Base behavioral or substance-use decisions on personal history and clinical assessment, not this marker

AA.effect: Two A1 alleles
AA.interpretation: Two A1 alleles are uncommon. Imaging studies report a population-level association with dopamine D2/3 receptor availability, while evidence for predicting addiction or personality remains inconsistent.
recommendations: Do not interpret this genotype as an addiction diagnosis or personality profile

sources.label: Taq1A and striatal dopamine receptor binding meta-analysis

limitations: Evidence for addiction and behavioral outcomes is inconsistent and not clinically predictive.
This locus is in ANKK1 and should not be presented as a deterministic DRD2 personality test.

CC.interpretation: Standard serotonin receptor 1A expression
recommendations: Standard stress response

CG.interpretation: One copy - may affect antidepressant response
recommendations: May respond differently to some SSRIs

GG.interpretation: G allele associated with increased risk of depression and suicide
recommendations: Stress management important
Consider CBT
Monitor mental health

3.interpretation: 3-repeat allele - lower enzyme activity, less efficient dopamine/serotonin breakdown
recommendations: Be aware of mood fluctuations
Stress management helps

4.interpretation: 4-repeat allele - higher enzyme activity, efficient neurotransmitter breakdown
recommendations: Standard dopamine/serotonin metabolism

5.interpretation: 5-repeat allele - highest activity, rapid neurotransmitter breakdown
recommendations: Standard mental health outlook
Standard mental health maintenance

GG.interpretation: Standard tryptophan hydroxylase 2 - normal serotonin synthesis
recommendations: Standard mood regulation

GT.interpretation: One copy - may have slightly reduced brain serotonin
recommendations: Omega-3 and tryptophan support may help

TT.interpretation: Associated with reduced brain serotonin and mood disorder susceptibility
recommendations: Prioritize mood-supporting lifestyle
Consider amino acid support
Monitor for mood changes
Standard reward and attention function

9/10.interpretation: One 9-copy allele - slightly increased dopamine in synapse
recommendations: May have slightly higher attention span

9/9.interpretation: 9-repeat homozygote - reduced dopamine reuptake, more dopamine in synapse
recommendations: May have better attention but also higher hyperactivity risk

GG.interpretation: Optimal oxytocin receptor sensitivity — enhanced empathy, social reward, and stress buffering from positive relationships; lower cortisol response to psychosocial stress
recommendations: Social connection is a key health lever for your genotype
Relationship quality strongly predicts health outcomes for GG carriers
Oxytocin-boosting activities: physical affection, group exercise, shared meals

AG.interpretation: Moderate oxytocin receptor expression — standard social bonding with some stress buffering capacity
recommendations: Cultivate close social relationships as part of health strategy

AA.effect: Lower OXTR expression
AA.interpretation: Reduced oxytocin receptor sensitivity — less social buffering of stress, stronger cortisol response to social stressors, loneliness has a larger impact on health outcomes
recommendations: Actively cultivate close relationships and community — the health impact of loneliness is larger for AA carriers
Mindfulness and compassion practices may compensate for lower social buffering
Outdoor and group exercise more beneficial than solo training
AA.scienceSimplified: Your oxytocin system is wired to be less reactive to social signals, which means the stress-buffering effect of relationships is less automatic — you benefit most from deliberately building social infrastructure

CC.interpretation: Standard endocannabinoid tone — normal anandamide breakdown, typical fear memory strength and stress reactivity
recommendations: Standard anxiety management: exercise, sleep, mindfulness

AC.interpretation: Moderately elevated endocannabinoid levels — somewhat blunted stress response, easier fear extinction
recommendations: Likely lower baseline anxiety than CC carriers
CBD supplementation has less added effect at moderate doses

AA.effect: Markedly reduced FAAH activity (bliss molecule effect)
AA.interpretation: Persistently elevated anandamide (the 'bliss molecule') — significantly reduced anxiety, stronger natural fear extinction, more resilient stress response, better placebo response; associated with lower endocannabinoid-related pain
recommendations: Your endocannabinoid system already runs at high tone — CBD supplementation redundant at typical doses
Exercise amplifies this advantage (exercise-induced anandamide surge)
Focus on fear memory extinction through exposure to novelty and challenge
AA.scienceSimplified: Your FAAH enzyme breaks down anandamide slowly — anandamide is your body's natural cannabis-like molecule that reduces anxiety and fear. You have a naturally elevated baseline of it.

AA.effect: Gly9/Gly9 — higher D3 receptor affinity for dopamine
AA.interpretation: Higher dopamine D3 receptor sensitivity — associated with greater impulsivity, novelty-seeking, and susceptibility to addiction and compulsive behaviors; D3 is highly expressed in limbic reward circuits
recommendations: Structure > spontaneity for high-stakes decisions (D3 high-affinity genotype responds more to immediate rewards)
Regular aerobic exercise — normalizes D3 receptor density
Mindfulness and meditation for impulse regulation

GA.interpretation: Intermediate dopamine D3 receptor sensitivity — partial impulsivity tendency
recommendations: Standard behavioral strategies for impulse management

GG.interpretation: Standard dopamine D3 signaling — normal reward-circuit impulsivity profile
recommendations: Standard cognitive health practices

GG.effect: CRH receptor variant — altered HPA axis stress reactivity
GG.interpretation: Corticotropin-releasing hormone receptor 1 variant — associated with increased risk for depression and anxiety under adversity; stress-sensitization phenotype; impaired CRH receptor feedback
recommendations: Stress resilience training is especially high-ROI: regular mindfulness, HRV biofeedback
CRH system is calmed by exercise — 150+ min/week aerobic is the most potent HPA axis regulator
Cognitive behavioral therapy shown to be more effective in CRHR1 risk genotypes (biological basis for resilience building)
Avoid chronic stress exposure; prioritize psychological safety in work/relationships

AG.interpretation: One variant allele — moderate HPA axis stress reactivity
recommendations: Stress management practices and aerobic exercise beneficial

AA.effect: Normal CRHR1 signaling
AA.interpretation: Wild-type CRH receptor — standard stress hormone feedback response
recommendations: Standard stress management

AA.effect: Reduced GAD67 expression — lower cortical GABA
AA.interpretation: Lower glutamate decarboxylase 1 expression — reduced cortical GABA synthesis; associated with anxiety phenotype, reduced cognitive inhibition, and higher risk for schizophrenia spectrum features in some studies
recommendations: GABA-supportive strategies: L-theanine 200mg (green tea, calming without sedation)
Magnesium glycinate 400mg — enhances GABA receptor function
Yoga and breath work directly increase GABA levels acutely
Low-glycemic diet — blood sugar volatility worsens GABA:glutamate balance
Taurine 1g — inhibitory amino acid that compensates for lower GABA

AG.interpretation: One variant allele — mildly reduced GABA synthesis
recommendations: L-theanine and magnesium for GABA support

GG.interpretation: Standard cortical GABA levels — normal inhibitory neurotransmitter balance
recommendations: Standard cognitive and mental health practices

GG.effect: Higher norepinephrine transporter expression — more efficient NE reuptake
GG.interpretation: Faster norepinephrine clearance from synapses — reduced noradrenergic tone; associated with lower reactivity to stress, calmer baseline state, potentially reduced ADHD symptoms
recommendations: Lower noradrenergic tone can mean lower alertness/drive in some contexts — aerobic exercise compensates
Caffeine and stimulant ergogenic effects may be reduced (lower NE target availability)
Standard neurological health practices

AA.effect: Lower NET1 expression — slower NE reuptake
AA.interpretation: Slower norepinephrine reuptake — elevated noradrenergic tone; associated with higher stress reactivity, better fight-or-flight response, and greater alertness/arousal
recommendations: Higher noradrenergic tone benefits from stress management training
HRV biofeedback and mindfulness directly downregulate NE system overactivation

TT.interpretation: Lower OXTR expression — reduced social bonding, empathy, and attachment behavior; associated with lower optimism and higher anxiety in some populations; relevant to social connection as a longevity predictor
recommendations: Deliberate social engagement routines — bonding activities upregulate oxytocin regardless of receptor density
Physical touch (massage, hugging with consent) acutely elevates oxytocin
Probiotics — gut microbiome modulates oxytocin production via vagus nerve
Avoid social isolation — particularly high-risk for this genotype

CT.interpretation: Moderate oxytocin receptor density — standard social bonding phenotype
recommendations: Social engagement and community participation beneficial

CC.effect: Higher OXTR expression — enhanced social bonding
CC.interpretation: Higher oxytocin receptor density — stronger social connection response, higher empathy, greater benefit from social relationships on stress and longevity
recommendations: Leverage high social sensitivity for wellbeing through strong community ties

GG.interpretation: Common genotype at the ABCA7 GWAS locus for Alzheimer disease
recommendations: No action needed

AG.effect: Heterozygous at ABCA7 — associated with Alzheimer disease
AG.interpretation: Carrier of one GWAS-significant allele at ABCA7 (n=532,679, p=4e-32). Associated with Alzheimer disease
recommendations: Heterozygous ABCA7 variant; moderate attention warranted

AA.effect: Homozygous effect allele at ABCA7 — Alzheimer disease association
AA.interpretation: Homozygous for effect allele at ABCA7. Strongest association with Alzheimer disease
recommendations: Note ABCA7 variant for Alzheimer disease; discuss with healthcare provider

AA.interpretation: Common genotype for Alzheimer disease at EPHA1; baseline trajectory
recommendations: No action needed; standard Alzheimer disease management applies

AG.effect: One EPHA1 protective allele — strong Alzheimer disease benefit
AG.interpretation: One copy of the GWAS-identified protective allele for Alzheimer disease. Strong benefit observed in population studies (n=383,378, p=8e-09)
recommendations: One protective EPHA1 allele — generally favorable

GG.effect: Homozygous EPHA1 protective allele — maximum Alzheimer disease benefit
GG.interpretation: Homozygous for the GWAS protective allele at EPHA1. Strongest favorable association with Alzheimer disease
recommendations: Favorable EPHA1 variant — continue healthy lifestyle to maximise this genetic advantage
